mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that suppress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal's out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a hit rate above 55%, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during expansion periods yields a negative or zero 5-day forward return, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when filtered to expansion-only samples.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 280.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 280.0, or the threshold effect shows no statistical edge over a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons confirms a resilient labor market that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll figures above 150,000 fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a hit rate above 52%, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with elevated inflation creates a stagflationary policy dilemma that forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high GDP and high inflation fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a contraction phase signals collapsing manufacturing demand and rising default risks, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during contraction periods yield positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 45% when restricted to contraction-only samples.
